summary: The claim that acute leukemia in children typically presents with gross gingival swelling is not well-supported; while gingival changes can happen, they're not characteristic compared to more reliable signs such as pallor and purpura. This makes Statement A misleadingly overstated and thus the exception among all options.

This makes Statement A misleadingly overstated and thus the exception among all options.. Answer choice: A
